join_order.probe: num(filtered: uom=USD, ddate BETWEEN 20230101 AND 20231231, value IS NOT NULL)
join_order.notes: pre_existence_hash mmapped read-only (402MB, capacity=33554432, load_factor=0.286). Software prefetch with P=16: for each qualifying row i, issue __builtin_prefetch(&pre_slots[hash3i(adsh[i+16], tag[i+16], ver[i+16]) & pmask], 0, 0) before processing row i. P=16 yields ~160ns head start (16 rows × ~10ns compute each), fully covering 70-100ns DRAM latency with margin vs P=8 which gave only 80ns coverage.

filters.method: USD is pre-seeded at index 0 in shared/uom.dict; hardcode usd_code=0, skip dict scan at runtime

data_structures.zonemap: flat_array(395 blocks × 10 bytes = 3.9KB, fits in L1; layout: [n_blocks:int32][uom_min:int8, uom_max:int8, ddate_min:int32, ddate_max:int32] per block)
data_structures.antijoin_lookup: pre_built_index(indexes/pre_existence_hash.bin, mmap 402MB, Robin Hood hash, capacity=33554432, load_factor=0.286, linear probe, empty=INT32_MIN); use MADV_RANDOM; software prefetch distance P=16
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(key=uint64_t packed (tag_code<<32|ver_code), value=(count:int64, sum:double), capacity=65536 per thread-local map, ~20K expected groups, sentinel=UINT64_MAX)
data_structures.dict_store: flat_buffer DictStore: struct { vector<char> data; vector<uint32_t> offsets; vector<uint16_t> lengths; } — replaces vector<string>. Eliminates 184K separate malloc() calls (93% of 198K tag strings are 47-byte avg, exceeding SSO threshold of ~22 bytes). Uses 3 large allocations total. Access: data.data()+offsets[i] for ptr, lengths[i] for len. Build: single sequential pass through dict file, reserve(total_bytes) once, then push_back each string's bytes.

filter_dimensions: uom: skip block if uom_max < usd_code OR uom_min > usd_code
ddate: skip block if ddate_max < 20230101 OR ddate_min > 20231231

parallelism.scan_parallelism: std::atomic<int32_t> block_cursor; threads steal blocks; each thread owns its thread-local HM
parallelism.antijoin_parallelism: pre_existence_hash is read-only; all threads probe concurrently, no synchronization needed
parallelism.merge_strategy: parallel_tree_reduction — log2(nthreads) rounds inside OMP parallel region with barrier between rounds (unchanged from iter_1)

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks on (uom, ddate); eliminates non-USD and out-of-2023 blocks before any column read
anti_join_lookup; replaces full pre table scan; O(1) average probe per num row with software prefetch P=16

decode: tag_code -> string via DictStore(shared/tag_numpre.dict): data.data()+offsets[tag_code], lengths[tag_code]
ver_code -> string via DictStore(shared/version_numpre.dict): data.data()+offsets[ver_code], lengths[ver_code]

optimization_notes: Two root causes addressed. (1) data_loading=23ms is dominated by 184K heap malloc() calls in load_dict for tag_numpre.dict: 93.1% of 198,311 tag strings average 47 bytes, well above the ~22-byte SSO threshold, so each triggers a separate heap allocation at ~100-150ns each = 18-25ms wasted. Fix (Category C): replace vector<string> with a flat DictStore — pre-reserve a single large buffer for all string bytes, store offsets and lengths as plain int arrays. This collapses 184K mallocs into 3 large allocations, cutting dict loading to ~1ms and data_loading from 23ms to ~3-5ms. Version strings (avg 20 bytes, 100% SSO-eligible) are fast and need no change. (2) main_scan prefetch P=8 gives only ~80ns head start against 70-100ns DRAM latency for probes into the 402MB pre_existence_hash — barely covering latency with no margin. Increasing to P=16 gives ~160ns head start, fully hiding DRAM latency and reducing probe stalls. Expected new total: ~58-62ms.
